title: Molten American Lamb Casserole
description: A garlicky, molten, one-pot American dish featuring star anise and a mother-sauce-base, perfect for a dairy-free friendly meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large terracotta dish, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the ground star anise, chopped carrots, chopped potatoes, tomato paste, vegetable broth, salt, and black pepper, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the dish with a lid.
Transfer the dish to the preheated oven and bake for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the vegetables are cooked through.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
Serve the casserole hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
